summarization.a: The line chart illustrates the normalized capacitance (C/Cox) against voltage for three different conditions: before thermal treatment, no barrier capacitor at 600°C, and Ti-Si-N (10 nm) capacitor at 800°C.

a.question: What are the capacitor characteristics of curves (a) and (b)?
a.answer: For both curves, the MOS capacitor does not have the barrier layer.

a.question: How does the normalized capacitance change at voltages above 1 V?
a.answer: For all the curves, the normalized capacitance exhibits the same behavior at voltage values above 1V: it remains constant as the voltage increases.

a.question: At what voltage value do the curves have the same normalized capacitance value?
How do the Cu atoms diffused onto Si can modify the C-V characteristics of the curve?
a.answer: The Cu atoms can enter the Si and form deep donor levels. These levels can act as the generation and recombination centers of electron-hole pairs.
